title: Is it a Magnet or Iron?
instructions_for_student: Drag each pole of the known magnet near the mystery bar's end. Observe attraction or repulsion to decide: is the mystery bar a magnet or just iron?

additionalProps.title: Is it a Magnet or Iron?
additionalProps.subtitle: Test the mystery bar to find out
additionalProps.summaryRule: Only repulsion proves something is a magnet.

rounds.conclusionLabel: This is iron — not a magnet
rounds.conclusionDetail: Both poles of the known magnet attracted the bar. No repulsion was observed, so it must be iron.

rounds.conclusionLabel: This is a magnet — repulsion detected!
rounds.conclusionDetail: One pole attracted and the other repelled the bar. Repulsion proves it is a magnet.

rounds.conclusionLabel: This is iron — not a magnet
rounds.conclusionDetail: Both poles attracted the bar. No repulsion means it cannot be identified as a magnet.

_tool_meta.subject: Science
_tool_meta.activity_reference: Activity 4.5: Let us experiment — A magnet can be identified by its property of repulsion
_tool_meta.icons: inline SVG only — no lucide-react or external icon libraries imported
_tool_meta.interaction_model: click-based sequential test per round (click N-pole test, then S-pole test, then reveal)

physics_logic.iron_bar: Both N and S poles of the known magnet attract iron — always 'attract' for both tests
physics_logic.magnet_bar: The known magnet repels the end of the mystery bar that has the same pole; attracts the opposite pole

agent_readme.description: A 3-round sequential investigation tool. Each round shows a grey 'mystery' bar and a known labelled magnet. The student clicks to test each pole (N then S) of the known magnet against the mystery bar's end. Green attract arrows or red repel arrows appear. After both tests, a 'Reveal Conclusion' button appears. The conclusion card shows whether it was iron (both attracted) or a magnet (one repelled). Revealed bar shows its true pole markings. After all rounds a summary screen shows per-round results and the key rule.

title.default: Is it a Magnet or Iron?

subtitle.default: Test the mystery bar to find out

summaryRule.description: Key rule shown at the bottom of the summary screen
summaryRule.default: Only repulsion proves something is a magnet.

rounds.description: Array of investigation rounds. Minimum 1, maximum any.

mysteryLabel.description: Label shown for the mystery bar (e.g. 'Mystery Bar A')

isMagnet.description: true = mystery bar is a magnet; false = mystery bar is iron

mysteryEndPole.description: Required only when isMagnet=true. The pole of the mystery bar at the tested end. The known magnet's same pole will repel this end.

conclusionLabel.description: Short conclusion shown in the conclusion card header

conclusionDetail.description: One-to-two sentence explanation shown in the conclusion card body

customisation_examples._comment: Single round quick demo — just the magnet case

rounds.conclusionDetail: The N-pole attracted the S-end and the S-pole repelled the S-end. Repulsion confirms a magnet.

customisation_examples._comment: 4-round extended version alternating iron and magnet

rounds.conclusionDetail: Both poles attracted. This is iron.
S-pole repelled; N-pole attracted. It's a magnet with S facing the known magnet.
Both poles attracted. This is iron.
N-pole repelled; S-pole attracted. Repulsion proves it's a magnet.

decision_tree.standard_3_rounds: Use the default rounds array — iron/magnet/iron sequence from NCERT Activity 4.5
decision_tree.single_round: Reduce to 1 round for a quick demonstration
decision_tree.more_rounds: Add more round objects — tool auto-adjusts progress dots and round count
decision_tree.change_mystery_pole: Set mysteryEndPole to 'N' or 'S' to control which pole of the mystery magnet is at the tested end
